channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Hidden supermassive black hole pairs may finally have a visible signal
item.description: Scientists propose a new method to detect tightly bound supermassive black hole pairs by observing stars that flash repeatedly.

item.title: Scientists discover a hidden quantum world inside cobalt
item.description: Advanced measurements reveal unexpected quantum complexity in cobalt, a metal previously considered fully understood.

item.title: Magnetic fields may be the key to rapid binary star formation
item.description: New supercomputer simulations suggest magnetic fields could explain how binary star systems form so quickly.

item.title: Octopuses use mirrors to find hidden food
item.description: Dartmouth researchers discovered that octopuses can learn to use mirrors to locate food they cannot directly see.
